Title: PLY (Python lex-Yacc)
URL: https://iditect.com/programming/python-example/ply-python-lex-yacc-an-introduction.html
Content: Title: PLY (Python lex-Yacc)
# PLY (Python lex-Yacc). PLY (Python lex-Yacc) is a pure-Python implementation of the popular Unix utilities lex and yacc. It's a tool for building parsers for custom languages. PLY provides a way to specify the lexical and syntactical structure of a language, and then generates Python code to parse this language. Here's a basic breakdown of how PLY works:. **Lexing with `ply.lex`**: This step involves converting a stream of characters (like a program's source code) into a stream of "tokens". Each token has a type, which is defined by regular expressions. **Parsing with `ply.yacc`**: This step takes the tokens generated by lexing and organizes them according to the grammar of the language, typically constructing a syntax tree or some other structured representation. ### Example:. Let's create a simple calculator that handles addition and multiplication using PLY:. First, we need to define our tokens. For our calculator, the tokens could be numbers, plus signs, and multiplication signs. After defining our tokens, we need to define the grammar of our language. In this case, the grammar for our calculator might look something like:. We can define this grammar in PLY as:. Now, we can test our calculator:. That's a simple introduction to PLY. In practice, you might deal with more complex tokens, grammar rules, and actions, but the fundamental approach remains the same. #### More Tags. #### More Programming Guides. #### Other Guides. #### More Programming Examples. #### Fitness Calculators. #### Auto Calculators. #### Financial Calculators. #### Date and Time Calculators. #### Internet Calculators. #### Pregnancy Calculators. #### Investment Calculators. #### Math Calculators. #### Housing/Building Calculators. #### Health Calculators. #### Retirement Calculators. #### Statistics Calculators. #### Various Measurements/Units Calculators. #### Everyday Utility Calculators. #### Weather Calculators. #### Real Estate Calculators. #### Tax and Salary Calculators. #### Geometry Calculators. #### Electronics/Circuits Calculators. #### Transportation Calculators. #### Entertainment/Anecdotes Calculators.

Title: Python Lex-Yacc (PLY) Overview | PDF | Parsing | Software - Scribd
URL: https://www.scribd.com/document/580844987/ply-intro
Content: # Python Lex-Yacc (PLY) Overview. ## Uploaded by. AI-enhanced title and description. PLY (Python Lex-Yacc) is a tool that allows users to define their own programming languages by specifying token definitions using regular expressions and grammars. It generates a lexer and parser from these specifications to act as a compiler for the language. The document provides an example of defining a simple calculator language with PLY and running it on the tux server, which already has PLY installed. It describes writing token and grammar rules, generating parsing tables, and testing the language implementation. ## Share this document. ## Footer menu. ## Support. ## Legal. ## Social. ## Get our free apps. Scribd - Download on the App Store. Scribd - Get it on Google Play.

Title: PLY (Python lex-Yacc) – An Introduction | Never Open Always Closed
URL: https://neveropen.tech/ply-python-lex-yacc-an-introduction
Content: Title: PLY (Python lex-Yacc) – An Introduction | Never Open Always Closed
HomeLanguagesPLY (Python lex-Yacc) – An Introduction. # PLY (Python lex-Yacc) – An Introduction. We all have heard of lex which is a tool that generates lexical analyzer which is then used to tokenify input streams and yacc which is a parser generator but there is a python implementation of these two tools in form of separate modules in a package called PLY. These modules are named lex.py and yacc.py and work similar to the original UNIX tools lex and yacc. **PLY** works differently from its UNIX counterparts in a way that it doesn’t require a special input file instead it takes the python program as inputs directly. The traditional tools also make use of parsing tables which are hard on compiler time whereas PLY caches the results generated and saves them for use and regenerates them as needed. This is one of the key modules in this package because the working of yacc.py also depends on lex.py as it is responsible for generating a collection of tokens from the input text and that collection is then identified using the regular expression rules. To import this module in your python code use **import ply.lex as lex**. When this is passed through ply.py, the following tokens are generated. #Token list of above tokens will be tokens = ('ID','EQUAL','ID', 'PLUS', 'NUMBER', 'TIMES','ID' ) #Regular expression rules for the above example t_PLUS = r'\+' t_MINUS = r'-' t_TIMES = r'\*' t_DIVIDE = r'/'. ### yacc.py. Another module of this package is yacc.py where yacc stands for **Yet Another Compiler Compiler**. It provides a lot of features that are already available in UNIX yacc and some extra features that give yacc.py some advantages over traditional yacc. You can use the following to import yacc into your python code **import ply.yacc as yacc**. The explicit token generation token() is also used by yacc.py which continuously calls this on user demand to collect tokens and grammar rules. Python implementation yacc.py doesn’t involve code-generation process instead it uses **reflection** to make its lexers and parsers which saves space as it doesn’t require any extra compiler constructions step and code file generation. #### Most Popular.

Title: PLY (Python lex-Yacc) - An Introduction - GeeksforGeeks
URL: https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/python/ply-python-lex-yacc-an-introduction
Content: We all have heard of lex which is a tool that generates lexical analyzer which is then used to tokenify input streams and yacc which is a parser generator but there is a python implementation of these two tools in form of separate modules in a package called PLY. These modules are named lex.py and yacc.py and work similar to the original UNIX tools lex and yacc. ### lex.py. This is one of the key modules in this package because the working of yacc.py also depends on lex.py as it is responsible for generating a collection of tokens from the input text and that collection is then identified using the regular expression rules. To import this module in your python code use **import ply.lex as lex**. You can use the following to import yacc into your python code **import ply.yacc as yacc**. For importing the tokens from your lex file use **from lex\_file\_name\_here import tokens** where tokens are the list of tokens specified in the lex file.
